content.problemStatement: Q6) A . Write different types of searching and sorting techniques. Explain Binary search in detail ?   ---3 Marks.
content.inputFormat: The first line contains an integer T (1 ≤ T ≤ 10), the number of test cases. Each of the next T lines contains a string (up to 100 characters) which may include spaces. The line may be empty.
content.outputFormat: Print the T strings in the same order, each on its own line, exactly as they were read.

sampleTestCases.input:
3
Binary Search
Linear Search
Bubble Sort
sampleTestCases.output:
Binary Search
Linear Search
Bubble Sort
sampleTestCases.explanation: The program reads T=3 and echoes each of the three technique names on its own line.
T=2, the two sorting technique names are printed unchanged.
Single test case; the string is output as‑is.

hidden.label: general case 1
hidden.input:
3
Binary Search
Quick Sort
Insertion Sort
hidden.expectedOutput:
Binary Search
Quick Sort
Insertion Sort

solution.approach: Read the integer T. Then loop T times, reading a whole line (including spaces) for each test case. Immediately output the line unchanged. No processing is required beyond correct line reading, because the task is to echo the input strings.
solution.timeComplexity: O(T * L) where L is the average length of a line (at most 100).
solution.spaceComplexity: O(L) for the buffer that holds one line at a time.

code.Python3:
import sys
data = sys.stdin.read().splitlines()
if not data:
    sys.exit()
T = int(data[0])
lines = data[1:1+T]
print('\n'.join(lines))
